rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes
Place implementation-level bindings in valid implementation scopes
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope
generic_rules: Preserve the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope to maintain clarity and avoid scope-related errors
Place assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes to ensure proper contract placement
Place implementation-level bindings in valid implementation scopes to ensure proper contract placement
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope; otherwise omit the unsafe clause
Use case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names for input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for the properties
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons or 0/1 equality over Boolean, enumeration-like, or opaque data ports
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
